qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multimodal prompt handling permits arbitrary local file read and SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable `ImageTextPromptValue` multimodal path as the advisory: attacker-controlled `retrieved_contexts` strings are treated as images based on filename-style MIME guessing and then opened or fetched without scheme safety validation.

selected_finding.title: Multimodal prompt handling permits arbitrary local file read and SSRF

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's root cause in `ImageTextPromptValue` and the copied PoC reproduced the advisory-described arbitrary local file read by leaking `/etc/passwd` through the generated multimodal data URL.
evidence: The advisory describes an arbitrary file read in `ImageTextPromptValue` caused by improper validation and sanitization of URL-like values supplied through `retrieved_contexts` during multimodal handling.

At vulnerable commit `0f412de0a4694680684b0981d08faae38152f80b`, `src/ragas/prompt/multi_modal_prompt.py` classifies attacker-controlled strings as images via `mimetypes.guess_type()`, routes `file://...#.jpg` through `get_image()`, and calls `urllib.request.urlopen()` or `open()` without scheme restrictions; fix commit `b28433709cbedbb531db79dadcfbdbd3aa6adcb0` replaces this with explicit security checks and disabled local file access by default.

The judge-side PoC run at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_13b9075a/.judge/poc_runs/multimodal_prompt_file_url_read/run.log` exited successfully and printed `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS` plus `root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash`.
The copied verifier only reports success after decoding the generated `data:` URL and confirming the leaked bytes match `/etc/passwd` byte-for-byte.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC generated a multimodal `data:` URL whose decoded bytes matched `/etc/passwd` exactly; `run.log` recorded `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS` and the leaked first line `root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash`.
